name: Shelf-Read a Section
description: Verify books are in correct Dewey Decimal order and fix misplaced items
steps: Start at the left end of the first shelf in the section.
Check that the Dewey number on each spine label is equal to or greater than the one before it.
If a book is out of order, pull it out and lay it on top of the shelf.
Continue shelf-by-shelf, working left to right, top shelf to bottom.
Once you reach the end of the section, return each pulled book to its correct position.
Report any book you cannot find a correct position for to the librarian.

name: Weeding Check
description: Identify books in a section that should be considered for removal from the collection
steps: Pull any book with a publication date more than 10 years old (check the copyright page inside the cover).
Check the circulation record sticker or system — if the book has not been checked out in 5+ years, flag it.
Inspect physical condition: torn cover, loose pages, water damage, or faded spine label — flag for withdrawal.
Place each flagged book on the weeding cart, face-up, with a sticky note showing the section (e.g., '630s').
Do NOT mark books as withdrawn yourself — leave that final decision to the librarian.
Record the count of flagged items on the shift log.

name: Pull a Hold
description: Locate a patron's reserved book and prepare it for pickup
steps: Find the hold request — note the patron's name and the book's full call number.
Go to the section indicated by the Dewey number on the hold slip.
Scan the shelf in call-number order until you find the matching title.
If found, place the hold slip inside the front cover and bring the book to the holds shelf.
Check the book in on the system so the patron's account shows it is ready for pickup.
If the book is not on the shelf, check the returns cart and sorting area; if still missing, notify the librarian to mark it as missing.

name: Sort the Returns Cart
description: Arrange returned books into shelf order before re-shelving
steps: Separate materials by collection: nonfiction, fiction, children's, large-print, and reference.
Within nonfiction, sort by Dewey Decimal number from lowest to highest (e.g., 001 before 813).
Within fiction, sort alphabetically by the author's last name.
Within children's, sort picture books alphabetically by author and easy readers by reading level.
Place each sorted group on a designated section of the cart, ready for shelving.
Re-shelve starting from the section closest to the returns desk to minimise travel time.

name: Withdrawn-Item Sweep
description: Remove any books stamped WITHDRAWN that are still on the open shelves
steps: Walk each aisle and look for books with a WITHDRAWN stamp on the page edges or cover.
Also check for books with a spine label marked 'W/D' or 'DISCARD'.
Pull each withdrawn item from the shelf and place it on the designated discard cart.
Do not place withdrawn items on the returns cart — they are not to be re-shelved.
Report the count and section to the librarian at the end of your sweep.
If a patron is holding a withdrawn item, politely explain it is not available and offer to help find an alternative.
